perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, "Confidential Information" shall include all information or material that has or could have commercial value or other utility in the business in which Disclosing Party is engaged. If Confidential Information is in writt en form, the Disclosing Party shall label or stamp the materials with the word "Confidential" or some similar warning. If Confidential Information is transmitted orally, the Disclosing Party shall promptly provide a writing indicating that such oral commun ication constituted Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, "Confidential Information" shall include all information or material that has or could have commercial value or other utility in the business in which Disclosing Party is engaged. Confidential information only includes materials explicitly labeled as such in writing and does not include oral communications. If Confidential Information is in written form, the Disclosing Party shall label or stamp the materials with the word "Confidential" or some similar warning. If Confidential Information is transmitted orally, the Disclosing Party shall promptly provide a writing indicating that such oral commun ication constituted Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds a sentence that significantly alters the definition of Confidential Information by stating 'Confidential information only includes materials explicitly labeled as such in writing and does not include oral communications.' This directly contradicts the following sentence which says, 'If Confidential Information is transmitted orally, the Disclosing Party shall promptly provide a writing indicating that such oral communication constituted Confidential Information.' This creates in-text contradiction and uncertainty regarding what constitutes Confidential Information, specifically if oral communications fall under the definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definition of Confidential Informat ion. For purposes of this Agreement, "Confidential Information" shall include all information or material that has or could have commercial value or other utility in the business in which Disclosing Party is engaged. Confidential information only includes materials explicitly labeled as such in writing and does not include oral communications. If Confidential Information is in writt en form, the Disclosing Party shall label or stamp the materials with the word "Confidential" or some similar warning. If Confidential Information is transmitted orally, the Disclosing Party shall promptly provide a writing indicating that such oral commun ication constituted Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'Confidential information only includes materials explicitly labeled as such in writing and does not include oral communications.' This directly contradicts the sentence 'If Confidential Information is transmitted orally, the Disclosing Party shall promptly provide a writing indicating that such oral communication constituted Confidential Information.' within the same text, creating a strong in-text contradiction, uncertainty, and potential unenforceability regarding the definition of Confidential Information and whether it includes oral communications.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Confidential Information to employees, contractors and third parties as is reasonably required and shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Confidential Information to employees, contractors and third parties as is reasonably required. The Receiving Party is not obligated to require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions, except in cases where required by law.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the Receiving Party 'shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement.' The added sentence now states 'The Receiving Party is not obligated to require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions, except in cases where required by law.' This directly contradicts the previous statement, creating uncertainty about the Receiving Party's obligations to ensure confidentiality among its personnel. This is an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Obligations of Receiving Party. Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Confidential Information in strictest confidence for the sole and exclusive benefit of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Confidential Information to employees, contractors and third parties as is reasonably required. The Receiving Party is not obligated to require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions, except in cases where required by law and shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement. Receiving Party shall not, without prior written approval of Disclosing Party, use for Receiving Party's own benefit, publish, copy, or otherwise disclose to others, or permit the use by others for their b enefit or to the detriment of Disclosing Party, any Confidential Information. Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any and all records, notes, and other written, printed, or tangible materials in its possession pertaining to Confidential Inform ation immediately if Disclosing Party requests it in writing.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Receiving Party 'shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement.' The changed text directly contradicts this by stating 'The Receiving Party is not obligated to require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions, except in cases where required by law.' This contradiction is mirrored in the 'Contradicted Text', which includes both the original requirement and the new exception, creating a clear in-text contradiction as outlined in the Perturbation Explanation and adhering to the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition. The change creates uncertainty about the Receiving Party's obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any and all records, notes, and other written, printed, or tangible materials in its possession pertaining to Confidential Inform ation immediately if Disclosing Party requests it in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any and all records, notes, and other written, printed, or tangible materials in its possession pertaining to Confidential Information within a reasonable timeframe, which will be determined on a case-by-case basis, if Disclosing Party requests it in writing.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates immediate return of materials related to Confidential Information upon written request. The modified text replaces 'immediately' with 'within a reasonable timeframe, which will be determined on a case-by-case basis.' This creates an in-text contradiction by introducing ambiguity and removing the explicit requirement for immediate action, thus contradicting the initial intent of prompt return.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Obligations of Receiving Party. Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Confidential Information in strictest confidence for the sole and exclusive benefit of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Confidential Information to employees, contractors and third parties as is reasonably required and shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement. Receiving Party shall not, without prior written approval of Disclosing Party, use for Receiving Party's own benefit, publish, copy, or otherwise disclose to others, or permit the use by others for their b enefit or to the detriment of Disclosing Party, any Confidential Information. Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any and all records, notes, and other written, printed, or tangible materials in its possession pertaining to Confidential Inform ation within a reasonable timeframe, which will be determined on a case-by-case basis, if Disclosing Party requests it in writing.
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'immediately,' while the changed text alters this to 'within a reasonable timeframe, which will be determined on a case-by-case basis.' The Contradicted Text is identical to the Changed text, however the changed text directly contradicts the intent of the original text, which demanded prompt return, by replacing 'immediately' with ambiguous terms. This ambiguity creates a direct contradiction because 'immediately' implies no delay, while 'reasonable timeframe' introduces a variable delay, creating an inconsistency as defined in the instructions. Because the text and contradicted text are in the same location, and that the changed text introduces ambiguity with the original intent, the changed text creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The nondisclosure provisions of this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement and Receiving Party's duty to hold Confidential Information in confidence shall r emain in effect until the Confidential Information no longer qualifies as a trade secret or until Disclosing Party sends Receiving Party written notice releasing Receiving Party from this Agreement, whichever occurs first.
perturbation.changed_text: The nondisclosure provisions of this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement; however, Receiving Party's duty to hold Confidential Information in confidence will automatically terminate one year after the agreement, regardless of trade secret status or written notice. Receiving Party's duty to hold Confidential Information in confidence shall r emain in effect until the Confidential Information no longer qualifies as a trade secret or until Disclosing Party sends Receiving Party written notice releasing Receiving Party from this Agreement, whichever occurs first.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the confidentiality obligations remain until the information is no longer a trade secret or the Disclosing Party releases the Receiving Party. By inserting 'Receiving Party's duty to hold Confidential Information in confidence will automatically terminate one year after the agreement, regardless of trade secret status or written notice', this introduces a conflicting term specifying a fixed one-year limit. This contradicts the provision that implies an indefinite duration as long as the information remains a trade secret.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Time Periods. The nondisclosure provisions of this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement; however, Receiving Party's duty to hold Confidential Information in confidence will automatically terminate one year after the agreement, regardless of trade secret status or written notice. Receiving Party's duty to hold Confidential Information in confidence shall r emain in effect until the Confidential Information no longer qualifies as a trade secret or until Disclosing Party sends Receiving Party written notice releasing Receiving Party from this Agreement, whichever occurs first.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a one-year limit on the confidentiality obligation, regardless of trade secret status or written notice. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, contains both the original clause stating the confidentiality obligation continues until the information is no longer a trade secret or the Disclosing Party releases the Receiving Party, and the added one-year limit. This directly contradicts the original clause because it implies the obligation could last longer than one year if the information remains a trade secret. Thus, the change creates a clear and direct conflict within the document, leading to a strong in-text contradiction. The perturbation type is Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction since the contradiction occurs in different parts of the same clause.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement expresses the complete understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter and supersedes all prior proposals, agreements, representations and understandings. This Agreement may not be amended except in a writing signed by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement expresses the complete understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter and supersedes all prior proposals, agreements, representations and understandings. This Agreement may not be amended except through mutual agreement, which may be demonstrated through written consent or inferred from the conduct of both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original states 'This Agreement may not be amended except in a writing signed by both parties.' By adding 'This Agreement may not be amended except through mutual agreement, which may be demonstrated through written consent or inferred from the conduct of both parties,' this allows for amendments not in writing, which contradicts the initial written amendment requirement. This creates uncertainty regarding how the agreement can be legally modified, an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Integration. This Agreement expresses the complete understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter and supersedes all prior proposals, agreements, representations and understandings. This Agreement may not be amended except through mutual agreement, which may be demonstrated through written consent or inferred from the conduct of both parties. This Agreement may not be amended except in a writing signed by both parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that amendments must be in writing and signed by both parties. The changed text introduces an alternative method for amendment through 'mutual agreement... inferred from the conduct of both parties,' which contradicts the initial requirement of a signed writing. This conflict is directly replicated in the contradicted text, which contains both amendment clauses, thus creating a clear contradiction within the document regarding how the agreement can be legally modified.

perturbation.original_text: The failure to exercise any right provided in this Agreement shall not be a waiver of prior or subsequent rights. This Agreement and each party's obligations shall be binding on the representatives, assigns and successors of such party.
perturbation.changed_text: The failure to exercise any right provided in this Agreement shall be deemed a waiver of that right. This Agreement and each party's obligations shall be binding on the representatives, assigns and successors of such party.
perturbation.explanation: The original states 'The failure to exercise any right provided in this Agreement shall not be a waiver of prior or subsequent rights.' The change inserts 'The failure to exercise any right provided in this Agreement shall be deemed a waiver of that right.' These two sentences are in direct contradiction, making uncertain whether rights are waived if not exercised.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Waiver. The failure to exercise any right provided in this Agreement shall be deemed a waiver of that right. This Agreement and each party's obligations shall be binding on the representatives, assigns and successors of such party. The failure to exercise any right provided in this Agreement shall not be a waiver of prior or subsequent rights.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that failure to exercise a right is deemed a waiver of that right, while the contradicted text, which includes the original phrasing 'The failure to exercise any right provided in this Agreement shall not be a waiver of prior or subsequent rights', directly conflicts with this by stating it's not a waiver. The change creates a direct and strong contradiction within the document as explained in the Perturbation Explanation.
